question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the effect of compounding frequency on the final investment value?

answer.text: Less frequent compounding always leads to higher returns
Compounding frequency has no impact on the final value
More frequent compounding leads to a higher final investment value
Annual compounding gives the highest return

explanation.text: **The Logic:** More frequent compounding (monthly vs annual) means returns are reinvested more often, each earning further returns. This results in a higher final value for the same nominal rate. **The Trap:** Option D is the opposite of the correct answer — annual compounding gives the LOWEST value among common frequencies. Daily > Monthly > Quarterly > Annual. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compounding frequency effects are tested in Series V-A (NAV computation), I (interest rate derivatives), and IV (fixed income products). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When comparing products, always check compounding frequency. A product offering 8% compounded monthly yields more than 8% compounded annually.
